review.correctedDescriptionShort: Profile of ex-Richmond Sockeyes defenceman Matt Hervey, crediting coach Orland Kurtenbach with turning his career around, as Hervey pursues a pro contract with the Winnipeg Jets' Moncton affiliate.
review.correctedDescriptionLong: The article profiles defenceman Matt Hervey, who credits Richmond Sockeyes coach Orland Kurtenbach — a former NHL player — with improving his attitude and reviving his hockey career. Hervey recorded 24 points in 17 regular-season games with the Sockeyes and was named to the first all-star team as Richmond won the Centennial Cup national junior-A championship at Humboldt, Saskatchewan. Now in Moncton on a 25-game amateur tryout with the Winnipeg Jets' affiliate, Hervey is playing under coach Rick Bowness and is second on the team in penalty minutes (47) in just six games, with a contract potentially pending.
review.notes: No headline is visible in the OCR — the AI-fabricated headline "Richmond Sockeyes Player Profile" should be removed. The article is from the Telegraph-Journal (Saint John, NB), which makes geographic sense given Hervey's Moncton connection. OCR renders "Humboldt" as "Humbolt" — the corrected descriptions use the proper spelling.
